recalls.Description: The recall pertains to LED gloves, specifically model ST01, which are available in small and medium sizes and feature various flashing modes. These gloves come with two pre-installed lithium coin batteries and an additional four batteries. The packaging displays "LED Gloves" on the top left side and specifies "Model ST01" on the bottom of the box.
recalls.Title: Recall of LED Gloves Due to Battery Ingestion Hazard; Non-Compliance with Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For inquiries, reach out to Minongad at yummyet2022@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled LED Gloves, Model ST01 – Packaging
Recalled LED Gloves, Model ST01 – Bottom of Package

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from Minongad between January 2025 and April 2025 for approximately $14.

Hazards.Name: The LED gloves do not meet the mandatory toy safety standards for coin batteries, as the battery compartment is easily accessible to children. Furthermore, the extra coin batteries lack child-resistant packaging and necessary warnings under Reese's Law. Ingesting button cell or coin batteries can lead to severe injuries, chemical burns, or even death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled LED gloves immediately, keep them away from children, and dispose of them properly. Contact Minongad for a full refund. Note: Dispose of button cell and coin batteries according to local hazardous waste guidelines.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of LED gloves due to battery ingestion risk; non-compliance with safety standards. Immediate action required for consumer safety.

recalls.Description: QVC has announced a recall of Temp-tations Oven Gloves, which were sold individually, in pairs, or as part of sets that included drying mats or trivets. These gloves were available in various colors such as blue, yellow, red, and floral prints, and feature the label "Temp-tations by Tara" stitched inside. The gloves are made from cotton and elastane and are offered in both small and large sizes. Model numbers for the recalled products include K51459, K76398, K47973, K48879, K85322, K96004, K92603, K308719, K309220, K309388, and K309516.
recalls.Title: QVC Issues Recall for Over One Million Temp-tations Oven Gloves Due to Burn Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the oven gloves immediately and reach out to QVC at 888-770-7119 from 8:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email ovengloves@realtimeresults.net or visit www.recallrtr.com/ovengloves and www.qvc.com, clicking on "Product Recall Info" at the bottom of the page for further details.

Injuries.Name: QVC has received 162 reports of insufficient heat protection including 92 reports of minor burns.

Retailers.Name: Available at QVC.com, QVC televised shows, and QVC digital shopping platforms from August 2018 through August 2024, priced between $4 and $13 per pair, and bundled sets with other kitchen items priced between $14 and $26.

Importers.Name: QVC Inc., of West Chester, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Insufficient heat protection leading to burn risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the gloves and contact QVC for a refund

recalls.metaDescription: QVC recalls Temp-tations Oven Gloves due to burn hazards. Stop using immediately and contact QVC for a refund.
